title: Miles Morales Actor Claims Venom Game Was Canceled After Tony Todd's Death—But Reporter Disputes It
summary:
A bombshell claim about a canceled Venom game has stirred up the gaming community. Nadji Jeter, the actor who portrays Miles Morales in Insomniac's Spider-Man series, revealed in an interview with Love it Film that a standalone Venom game and downloadable content (DLC) were in the works but scrapped after the passing of Tony Todd, the actor who voiced Venom in Spider-Man 2. "We were going to have a Venom game. We were going to drop a Venom game and a Venom DLC, but we lost Tony Todd…" Jeter said. He added that Insomniac showed him how the game would start.

However, Bloomberg reporter Jason Schreier quickly disputed Jeter's account on ResetEra, simply stating, "This isn't true." It's unclear whether Schreier meant the game was never canceled or that Todd's death wasn't the reason. The confusion deepens when considering leaked documents from the 2023 Insomniac hack, which revealed that the studio had been working on a standalone Venom game for some time, with a tentative roadmap targeting a 2025 release. Yet, as of 2026, no such game has been announced. Insomniac is currently preparing to ship Marvel's Wolverine this September, and other projects like Spider-Man 3, a new Ratchet & Clank, and an X-Men game are also rumored.

Given the studio's packed schedule, it's uncertain when—or if—a Venom game will materialize. But the leaked documents suggest Insomniac saw it as a way to bridge the gap between Spider-Man 2 and Spider-Man 3, so the idea may not be dead yet.
why_it_matters: This story reveals how behind-the-scenes events—like leaks, deaths, and conflicting reports—can fan expectations and create controversy in the gaming world. It also illustrates the challenge of managing multiple huge-budget projects and the importance of waiting for official announcements before believing rumors.

keywords.explanation: Shocking news that surprises many people.
Separate and not part of a larger series.
Argued against or claimed to be false.
Not final and subject to change.

questions.question: Who claimed that a Venom game was canceled after Tony Todd's death?
According to leaked documents, what was the tentative release year for the Venom game?
Which game is Insomniac currently preparing to ship in September?
What role did Tony Todd play in Spider-Man 2?
What did the leaked documents suggest the Venom game would bridge?

background_read: Insomniac Games is the studio behind the acclaimed Marvel's Spider-Man series for PlayStation. In 2023, the studio suffered a major ransomware attack that leaked internal documents, including plans for future games. Among those was a standalone Venom game with a tentative 2025 release, intended to bridge the story between Spider-Man 2 and Spider-Man 3.

Tony Todd (an actor famous for his deep voice and horror roles, especially the villain Candyman) voiced Venom in Spider-Man 2. His passing in late 2024 was widely mourned by the gaming community. Nadji Jeter (the voice actor for Miles Morales in the same games) claimed in an interview that the Venom game was cancelled after Todd's death.

However, Bloomberg reporter Jason Schreier, known for accurate video game scoops, disputed Jeter's account without giving details. This highlights how conflicting information can emerge from leaks, interviews, and official silence. As of 2026, Insomniac has not announced any Venom game; they are focused on Marvel's Wolverine, set for September 2026, with other projects rumored.

The situation shows the complexity of game development: plans change, actors die, and what appears in leaked documents may not reflect current reality. Fans remain hopeful that a Venom game could still happen after Insomniac's upcoming releases.
Article_Structure: LEAD: Nadji Jeter claims Venom game canceled after Tony Todd's death
└─ JETER'S QUOTE: 'We were going to have a Venom game and DLC... but we lost Tony Todd'
COUNTER: Bloomberg reporter Jason Schreier says 'This isn't true'
└─ UNCERTAINTY: unclear if game never existed or if cancellation had other reasons
EVIDENCE: 2023 Insomniac hack leaked documents showed Venom game with 2025 tentative roadmap
└─ PURPOSE: leaked docs said it would bridge Spider-Man 2 and 3
CURRENT SLATE: Insomniac preparing Marvel's Wolverine for September; Spider-Man 3, Ratchet & Clank, X-Men rumored
OPEN QUESTION: Venom game may still be possible; schedule is packed but demand remains high

perspectives.description: If a Venom game exists in some form, fans of the character have reason to be excited. The leaked roadmap suggests Insomniac considered it seriously, so it might still happen after Wolverine.
The cancellation claim is disappointing, especially losing Tony Todd's performance. The dispute creates confusion and distrust among fans who don't know whom to believe.
Whether true or not, this situation is typical of game development: many projects are planned but only a few are released. The 2023 hack shows only a snapshot in time, and plans can change.

Even if the Venom game is currently shelved, the robust demand and the character's popularity make a future release likely after Insomniac's current slate clears. The dispute may pressure Insomniac to address the rumors officially.
